work_experience.company: Shanghai NEW TOUCH software developing company
responsibilities: Designed an internal management web for a local bank, focusing on usability and operational efficiency.
Proposed and implemented front-end solutions using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
Assisted in testing and validating SQL database integration and functionality.

projects.name: NewsieAI
projects.description: End-to-end agentic news automation platform built as a solo engineer, covering architecture, backend APIs, frontend workflow UI, and deployment. Implemented a dual-agent system for interactive profile-building and retrieval-driven content discovery. Built modular MCP tool services (search, payment, profile management, geo/time) and integrated them into agent orchestration for reliable task execution. Implemented an x402-compatible payment flow on Solana with payment request handling, transaction verification, and gated content re-access. 100+ stars on GitHub. Collaborators: Prof. Yue Li (XJTLU), Prof. Yulin Liu (ETH Zurich).

projects.name: LLM-Powered Multi-Purpose Agent Applications on Discord
projects.description: Developed LLM-embedded agent applications for Discord. Bot 1: Role-playing bot for customizable, real-time character adaptation and emotional support using context engineering and dual-model coupling for dynamic persona control and memory. Integrated LoRA fine-tuning, async prompt distillation, and iterative injection to enhance performance while reducing API costs by 20–30%. Bot 2: RAG-based game assistant using Sentence-Transformer and ChromaDB to retrieve semantically relevant game lore and mechanics, with re-ranking and document argumentation. Revenue: 22k CNY via subscription.

projects.name: Digital Corpse Donator: Caring Digital Burial Process
projects.description: Conceptualized and designed an interactive experience where users curate, revise, and donate posthumous data via digital interfaces to create social impact. Led an activity-based workshop and user research (n=41), employing interviews, directed storytelling, cognitive walkthroughs, and quantitative analysis. Client: Axa Health UK, London.

projects.name: Lumen: A Light Management Platform
projects.description: Conducted market and user research on light intake and management; produced analytic reports guiding product development and marketing. Designed and led a pilot study and workshops, defining core concepts and design principles based on the Light-Time Impact model and created a Semi-Swiss-Cheese assessment model to estimate risk of Seasonal Affective Disorder. Explored innovative interfaces for human-light communication (e.g., light-audio spectrum transition, algae-oxygen based light translation). Client: Tala Light UK, London.

projects.name: D-IFSC: Interactive Visualization of IFSC Sport Climbing Competition and Athlete Data
projects.description: Built an interactive sport climbing data platform (HTML/CSS/JavaScript) improving accessibility and engagement across diverse demographics. Independently extracted, pre-processed, and analyzed athlete data (n=1683, p ≤ 15) using Python and Selenium. Designed and iteratively optimized domain-specific visualizations with Tableau and amCharts JS. User study (N=23) showed significant improvements over baseline in aesthetics (↑1.47), usability (↑1.14), and usefulness (↑1.61), all p < 0.001.

projects.name: UnIEM: Distributed User Interface Framework for IoT Multi-Device Environments
projects.description: Developed UnIEM in Java, an interaction model for real-time reconfiguration of Distributed User Interfaces across dynamic IoT environments. Configured core framework functionality with modular structure allowing adaptive UI distribution based on user preferences, accessibility, and context. Built a mobile app for video migration on UnIEM and validated effectiveness through experiments simulating educational scenarios.

projects.name: OneClick
projects.description: Android app inspired by iOS Shortcuts allowing users to customize shortcuts for quick access to apps and actions. Architected with OOP principles; implemented gesture recognition and floating windows; designed data structures for storing shortcuts enabling lightweight, real-time management. Employed participatory design with questionnaires, interviews, and observations to enhance usability.

projects.name: TCP-based LEFT File Sharing Protocol in Python
projects.description: Engineered a custom TCP-based file synchronization protocol in Python, featuring optimized buffer management, break-point detection, and stateful session handling to maximize throughput and ensure data integrity. Scored 97/100 in stress-testing. Applied OOP for core components (File Scanner, Listener, Downloader) and developed a custom header system for file chunking and error detection.

projects.name: Torch-Vibration Feedback Guiding Glove
projects.description: Infrared-based physical system converting distance data into nonlinear vibrotactile feedback to assist users preferring discreet assistive devices. Optimized haptic signal intuitiveness under complex conditions via user research. Implemented with Arduino and an asynchronous monitoring system that adjusts sampling based on oscillation amplitude.

projects.name: Maple Valley (VR)
projects.description: Immersive VR game emphasizing exploration, gathering, crafting, and building. Implemented a VR-adaptive inventory interaction system using position-based detection to enable intuitive, low-friction control aligned with natural behaviors.

publications_or_talks: Interactive Visualization of Sport Climbing Data. INTERACT 2023 - IFIP Conference on Human-Computer Interaction 2023. Authors: Fangze Qiu; Yue Li. Aug 2023.
Digital Corpse Donator: Caring Digital Burial Process. Chinese CHI (year not fully specified). Authors: Fangze Qiu; Yue Li. Dec (year not fully specified).

A Re-configurable Interaction Model in Distributed IoT Environment. CyberC 2022 - International Conference on Cyber-Enabled Distributed Computing and Knowledge Discovery. Authors: Fangze Qiu; Huaxiao Huang; Yuji Dong. Oct 2022.

Email domain appears truncated (outlook.co) and a UK phone number seems incomplete; both retained as-is. Locations inferred only when explicitly present (e.g., 'London' in institution name/clients). Technology stacks listed only when explicitly mentioned.
